The Estate

A single estate.
Three revenue streams.
One integrated system.

Hacienda Labu is a privately held agroforestry estate in Siuna and Bonanza, within the buffer zone of the Bosawás UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. Since 2010, we have replanted degraded cattle land — generating three complementary, mutually reinforcing revenue streams: certified teak, fine flavour cacao, and verified carbon credits. Roughly a third of the estate is kept in permanent conservation.

Growing

From our current 5,024 managed hectares, we are on track to reach 6,500 hectares within the next few years — as the case for agroforestry continues to demonstrate itself on the ground.

Truly Sustainable

Teak reaches its first final harvest in 2030. After harvest, those lands are replanted immediately — beginning the cycle again. A permanently productive, self-renewing system designed to operate, sequester carbon, and generate livelihoods in a sustainable manner.

5,024Hectares total — plantations, conservation, and restoration across Siuna & Bonanza
2010Planting started — 15+ years of continuous agroforestry operation
450+Direct employees — 95% from local communities, 100% Nicaraguan
3Independent certifications — FSC® C108572 · Rainforest Alliance · Verra VCS 2365
6,500Ha planned — expansion target within the next few years
Certified Teak · Sustainable
The queen
of hardwoods.

FSC®-certified plantation teak from 3,860 ha. Kiln-dried, consistent, EUDR-compliant. S4S and custom milling.

15 containers/month → 60 by 2030
Explore Teak
Fine Flavour Cacao
Two named profiles.
Custom on demand.

Rainforest Alliance certified. Shade-grown under certified teak. Two named profiles — Labu and Chumilo — with custom profiles developed to buyer specification.

1,100 ha · 350 MT growing to 1,500 MT

The Asocio System · Siuna, RACCN
The Asocio System

The idea that
makes everything work.

The asocio method — certified cacao grown beneath the canopy of certified teak — is why our cacao has its complexity and flavour, why our carbon credits are high quality, and why biodiversity thrives across the estate.

🪵
Teak — upper canopy, primary carbon layer, sustainable harvest in 2030 and beyond
🍫
Cacao — mid canopy, shade-grown complexity, Labu & Chumilo profiles · custom on demand
🌿
Conservation — 1,576 ha buffer zone, 920+ species, Bosawás Reserve
